label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. I need to figure out which sentence is not true based on the article about Bobby Moresco. First, let's go through each option one by one. Option A says, "His father did not support his work as a bartender." Wait, in the article, it mentions that when he moved to Hollywood, he worked as a waiter. The father said, "Stop this craziness and get a job; you have a wife and daughter." So his father wanted him to get a job, which implies that the father didn't support his acting career. But the option says he didn't support his work as a bartender. But in the article, he was a waiter, not a bartender. Wait, maybe the option has a mistake here. The original article says he worked as a waiter, not a bartender. So if the option says "bar tender," that's incorrect. So A might be the false one. Option B: "Before he became an overnight success, his life experienced ups and downs." The article describes his struggles in the 70s making $2,000 over a decade, moving to Hollywood, working as a cab driver and waiter, then moving back after his brother's death, writing, then getting work again, and then going out of work again in 2003 before Crash. So yes, his life had many ups and downs before success. So B is true. Option C: "His brother's death inspired his writing Half-Deserted Streets." The article says that after his brother's murder in 1983, he started writing as a way to explore the pain of Hell's Kitchen, and the play was based on his brother's killing.
